arxiv.title: UPAR: A Kantian-Inspired Prompting Framework for Enhancing Large Language Model Capabilities
arxiv.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive inferential capabilities, with numerous research endeavors devoted to enhancing this capacity through prompting. arxiv.title: A Brief History of Prompt: Leveraging Language Models. (Through Advanced Prompting)
arxiv.abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive exploration of the evolution of prompt engineering and generation in the field of natural language processing (NLP). arxiv.title: Unveiling the Unborn: Advancing Fetal Health Classification through Machine Learning
arxiv.abstract: Fetal health classification is a critical task in obstetrics, enabling early identification and management of potential health problems. However, it remains challenging due to data complexity and limited labeled samples. This research paper presents a novel machine-learning approach for fetal health

arxiv.title: Self-Supervised Open-Ended Classification with Small Visual Language Models
arxiv.abstract: We present Self-Context Adaptation (SeCAt), a self-supervised approach that unlocks few-shot abilities for open-ended classification with small visual language models. arxiv.title: From Language Modeling to Instruction Following: Understanding the Behavior Shift in LLMs after Instruction Tuning
arxiv.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable success, where instruction tuning is the critical step in aligning LLMs with user intentions. arxiv.title: Generative Design of inorganic compounds using deep diffusion language models
arxiv.abstract: Due to the vast chemical space, discovering materials with a specific function is challenging. arxiv.title: Learning Informative Latent Representation for Quantum State Tomography
arxiv.abstract: Quantum state tomography (QST) is the process of reconstructing the complete state of a quantum system (mathematically described as a density matrix) through a series of different measurements. arxiv.title: Enhancing Efficiency and Privacy in Memory-Based Malware Classification through Feature Selection
arxiv.abstract: Malware poses a significant security risk to individuals, organizations, and critical infrastructure by compromising systems and data. arxiv.title: SimLVSeg: Simplifying Left Ventricular Segmentation in 2D+Time Echocardiograms with Self- and Weakly-Supervised Learning
arxiv.abstract: Echocardiography has become an indispensable clinical imaging modality for general heart health assessment. arxiv.title: On the Role of Neural Collapse in Meta Learning Models for Few-shot Learning
arxiv.abstract: Meta-learning frameworks for few-shot learning aims to learn models that can learn new skills or adapt to new environments rapidly with a few training examples.
